finding: User access review is defined conceptually, but there is no consistent quarterly sign-off record for privileged access review.
remediation_action: Formalize quarterly user access review with reviewer sign-off, exception tracking, removal evidence, and management approval.
evidence_required: User-role export, access review worksheet, exception list, removal confirmation, reviewer sign-off
closure_criteria: Quarterly access review is completed with reviewer sign-off, exception list, removal evidence, and management approval.
dependency: User-role export availability and management reviewer assignment
expected_management_outcome: Privileged access is periodically validated and unauthorized access exposure is reduced.
source_reference: NIST CSF PR.AA/GV.RR/GV.OV; NIST SP 800-53 AC/IA; CIS Account Management; COBIT governance concepts; Indonesia PDP Law
notes: Simulated POA&M item created from Step 5 control assessment result and Step 6 gap analysis.

finding: Access approval evidence exists for some requests, but lifecycle reconciliation and removal evidence are incomplete.
remediation_action: Implement a monthly joiner-mover-leaver reconciliation and require access removal evidence for role changes and terminations.
owner: IT Admin / HR Coordinator / Data Owner
evidence_required: Access request form, approval record, active user list, HR role-change list, deactivation evidence
closure_criteria: Joiner-mover-leaver reconciliation is completed, exceptions are resolved, and access removal evidence is retained.
dependency: HR role-change list and active user export reconciliation
expected_management_outcome: Sensitive access is aligned with current job responsibility and user lifecycle events.
source_reference: NIST CSF PR.AA/PR.DS/GV.PO; NIST SP 800-53 AC/IA/PT; CIS Data Protection; COBIT governance concepts; Indonesia PDP Law
notes: Simulated POA&M item created from Step 5 control assessment result and Step 6 gap analysis.

finding: System logs are assumed to exist, but there is no documented monthly admin activity log review or exception escalation evidence.
remediation_action: Define a monthly admin log review procedure, exception criteria, reviewer responsibility, and escalation workflow.
evidence_required: Admin activity log extract, log review checklist, exception register, reviewer sign-off, follow-up ticket
closure_criteria: Monthly admin log review procedure is approved, first review is completed, exceptions are documented, and escalation evidence is retained.
dependency: Admin log extraction capability and exception escalation owner
expected_management_outcome: Privileged activity monitoring becomes reviewable and management can detect unusual admin behavior earlier.
source_reference: NIST CSF DE.CM/GV.OV; NIST SP 800-53 AU; CIS Audit Log Management; COBIT monitoring concept
notes: Simulated POA&M item created from Step 5 control assessment result and Step 6 gap analysis.

finding: Backup activity is assumed, but there is no documented restoration test evidence to confirm recovery readiness.
remediation_action: Perform quarterly backup restoration testing and document test scope, result, issues, remediation, and sign-off.
evidence_required: Backup schedule, restore test log, restoration screenshot, recovery result, issue log, management sign-off
closure_criteria: Restore test is performed, results are documented, issues are tracked, and IT Operations sign-off is recorded.
dependency: Backup scope confirmation and restore test environment availability
expected_management_outcome: Recovery readiness is validated and backup reliability is supported by evidence.
source_reference: NIST CSF RC.RP/PR.DS/ID.IM; NIST SP 800-53 CP; ISO 27001 availability concept; CIS Data Recovery; COBIT continuity concept
notes: Simulated POA&M item created from Step 5 control assessment result and Step 6 gap analysis.

finding: Change approval exists for some changes, but UAT evidence and rollback documentation are inconsistent.
remediation_action: Standardize change request documentation, UAT sign-off, rollback plan, and post-implementation review before production release.
evidence_required: Change request form, approval record, UAT result, rollback plan, release note, post-change review
closure_criteria: Change request, UAT sign-off, rollback plan, and post-implementation review are attached for sampled production changes.
dependency: Change request template, UAT checklist, and change owner adoption
expected_management_outcome: System changes become traceable and service disruption risk from undocumented changes is reduced.
source_reference: NIST CSF PR.PS/GV.OV; NIST SP 800-53 CM/SA; CIS Secure Configuration; COBIT BAI06 managed IT changes concept
notes: Simulated POA&M item created from Step 5 control assessment result and Step 6 gap analysis.

finding: Incident tickets are available, but severity classification and escalation evidence are not consistently documented.
remediation_action: Define an incident severity matrix, escalation SLA, required ticket fields, and post-incident review checklist.
evidence_required: Incident ticket, severity classification, escalation log, communication note, resolution record, post-incident review
closure_criteria: Incident severity matrix, escalation SLA, required ticket fields, and post-incident checklist are approved and used in sample tickets.
dependency: Helpdesk ticket field configuration and severity definition agreement
expected_management_outcome: Incident handling becomes more consistent and escalation decisions become auditable.
source_reference: NIST CSF RS.MA/RS.CO/RS.AN; NIST SP 800-53 IR; CIS Incident Response Management; COBIT service request/incident concept
notes: Simulated POA&M item created from Step 5 control assessment result and Step 6 gap analysis.

finding: Vendor dependency is recognized, but SLA review and escalation testing are not consistently documented.
remediation_action: Maintain vendor SLA review records, escalation contacts, support scope, and periodic dependency review notes.
evidence_required: Vendor SLA document, support contact list, escalation matrix, review meeting note, issue resolution record
closure_criteria: Vendor SLA review record, escalation contacts, support scope, and dependency review notes are documented.
dependency: Vendor contract/SLA documentation and escalation contact confirmation
expected_management_outcome: Critical vendor dependency is monitored and escalation readiness is improved.
source_reference: NIST CSF GV.SC/GV.OV; NIST SP 800-53 SR; CIS Service Provider Management; COBIT APO10 vendor management concept
notes: Simulated POA&M item created from Step 5 control assessment result and Step 6 gap analysis.

finding: No formal retention schedule or deletion review evidence is available for student-related personal data.
remediation_action: Define data retention rules, deletion review process, data owner approval, and evidence documentation for personal data lifecycle governance.
evidence_required: Data retention schedule, retention review worksheet, deletion approval, exception register, policy review note
closure_criteria: Retention schedule, deletion review process, approval record, and evidence log are created for student-related personal data.
dependency: Data owner approval and retention category definition
expected_management_outcome: Personal data retention becomes governed and privacy compliance readiness improves.
source_reference: NIST CSF PR.DS/GV.PO/GV.OC; NIST SP 800-53 PT/MP; ISO 27001 confidentiality concept; Indonesia PDP Law
notes: Simulated POA&M item created from Step 5 control assessment result and Step 6 gap analysis.

finding: Training may exist informally, but completion records and role-specific privacy/security awareness evidence are incomplete.
remediation_action: Create annual awareness training completion tracking and onboarding awareness checklist for admin users handling sensitive data.
evidence_required: Training material, attendance/completion report, quiz result, reminder notice, exception follow-up
closure_criteria: Annual training tracker and onboarding awareness checklist are created, and completion records are retained for admin users.
dependency: Training material ownership and HR training tracker setup
expected_management_outcome: Users handling sensitive data receive traceable privacy and security awareness coverage.
source_reference: NIST CSF PR.AT/GV.RR; NIST SP 800-53 AT; CIS Security Awareness and Skills Training; COBIT APO07 people/skills concept
notes: Simulated POA&M item created from Step 5 control assessment result and Step 6 gap analysis.

finding: Data validation is performed in some cases, but reconciliation evidence and exception resolution tracking are not standardized.
remediation_action: Standardize monthly reporting validation checklist, reconciliation steps, exception owner, and reviewer sign-off.
owner: BI / Reporting Owner / Finance Data Owner
evidence_required: Validation checklist, reconciliation report, exception log, correction record, dashboard refresh note
closure_criteria: Monthly validation checklist, reconciliation evidence, exception log, and reviewer sign-off are completed for key reports.

expected_management_outcome: Reporting quality improves and management decisions become less exposed to unresolved data issues.
source_reference: NIST CSF ID.IM/PR.DS; NIST SP 800-53 SI; ISO 27001 integrity concept; COBIT information quality concept

finding: Continuity need is recognized, but walkthrough/testing evidence and fallback ownership are not fully documented.

closure_criteria: Continuity procedure is documented, pre-peak walkthrough is completed, fallback owners are assigned, and action items are tracked.

finding: Workflow records exist, but closure evidence and sample review results are not consistently linked to each request.
remediation_action: Require closure evidence, approval note, reviewer field, and monthly sample review for document request fulfillment.

finding: No formal vulnerability backlog or patch tracking evidence is available for in-scope assets.
remediation_action: Create a vulnerability backlog linked to asset inventory, severity, owner, due date, remediation status, and closure evidence.

closure_criteria: Vulnerability backlog is created with asset mapping, severity, owner, due date, remediation status, and closure evidence.

finding: Personal data export review control is planned but not yet implemented in the simulated control environment.
remediation_action: Implement approval and log review for bulk personal data exports, including purpose, scope, requester, approver, and retention note.

closure_criteria: Bulk personal data export approval and log review are implemented with requester, purpose, approver, retention note, and review evidence.

expected_management_outcome: Personal data export activity becomes more accountable and privacy risk is reduced.

finding: Centralized control evidence repository is planned but not yet implemented.
remediation_action: Build an evidence tracker and repository index to centralize evidence ownership, review status, and management reporting.

closure_criteria: Evidence repository index is created with control owner, evidence status, review date, reviewer, and management reporting fields.
dependency: Repository location, evidence naming convention, and control owner onboarding
expected_management_outcome: Control evidence becomes centralized, easier to review, and more audit-ready.
source_reference: NIST CSF GV.OV/GV.RM/ID.IM; NIST SP 800-53 CA/PL; NIST SP 800-53A assessment concepts; COBIT MEA monitoring concepts
